title: execute_enter_worktree + execute_exit_worktree mutate process CWD — global side effect breaks thread safety
description:
**Dimension:** Structural Modularity (Temporal Coupling + Global State) / Error Orchestration

**Location:** src/tools/worktree.rs (originally lines 102, 134, 213, 244, 255)

**Original violation:** execute_enter_worktree called std::env::set_current_dir(&worktree_dir) after creating a git worktree (and three further sites in exit). Process-wide CWD mutation races against every other thread doing relative-path work — POSIX, Rust, and Go all document chdir as fundamentally unsafe for concurrent processes.

# Phase 1 — LANDED (this issue)

- execute_enter_worktree no longer mutates process CWD on ANY path. It returns the new worktree path in its success message.
- execute_exit_worktree now requires an explicit 'path' argument and no longer reads CWD. Tool schema updated accordingly.
- All git invocations route through git_in(cwd, args) which uses Command::current_dir(cwd) so no git subprocess depends on parent CWD either.
- 5 new forensic tests (including a compile-time anti-regression test that greps the production region for set_current_dir( calls).
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: 0 errors. cargo test --lib: 1277/1277.

# Phase 2 — deferred to #993

- Thread the active worktree path through ToolContext.cwd: Option<&Path>; bash/file/lsp tool handlers consume it.
- Persist WorktreeState on ChatSession; populate from enter_worktree's structured return.
- Add WorktreeGuard / Drop-based cleanup to remove leaked worktrees on session crash.
- Item #4 from the original refactor list (no `let _ =` on cleanup) is partially addressed — worktree removal now surfaces failures to the caller; the remaining best-effort sites are inside helpers and produce honest error tuples.
